sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal. Aram. and Arabic to cleave, to divide (comp. as to the primary stock under the root פָּלָא). Hence-

Niphal, to be divided, Gen 10:25 1Ch 1:19.

Piel, to divide, as a channel, watercourse, Job 38:25. Metaph. Psa 55:10, “divide their tongue,” cause them to fall out amongst themselves.

Derivatives, פֶּלֶג-פְּלֻגָּה, מִפְלַגָּה.
